projects.title: StudyExe
projects.description: A study app for people with ADHD. Features body doubling, eye tracking (warns after 5s looking away), Pomodoro timer, screen lock, and AI-scored timed recall sessions. Built in Python with a custom GUI.
projects.blogContent:
StudyExe is a focused study application I built specifically for people with ADHD — including myself. The core idea is simple: remove every possible distraction and force deep focus using science-backed techniques.

The app takes over your entire screen when a study session begins, making it impossible to switch to other apps. You upload PDF files or text-based study material, and the app locks you in with a timer.

One of the standout features is real-time eye tracking. If you look away from the screen for more than 5 seconds, a loud alert plays to bring your attention back. This acts as a digital body-doubling mechanism — it's like having someone watching over your shoulder to keep you accountable.

After each study session, you're prompted to write down everything you remember from the material within a time limit. You can peek at the material, but this speeds up the timer and penalizes your score. Once submitted, the text is scored by AI (OpenAI API), giving you an objective measure of how much you retained.

The app also includes a rapid-fire questions module optimized for keyboard shortcuts, letting you blaze through multiple-choice questions. All wrong answers are collected for later review.

Over time, StudyExe tracks your scores, study hours, and correct answers — giving you a clear picture of your progress. It's been a game-changer for my own studies and I hope it helps others too.

screenshots.description: The core study session experience, utilizing an Obsidian-inspired theme. This workflow guides users through setting up a session, actively reading materials while being monitored, and finally completing a timed recall test to assess retention.
StudyExe allows you to customize your environment to fit your mood or focus style, featuring dynamic themes such as Cyberforest, Red Cyberpunk, and Daylight.
The online portal for StudyExe where users can explore features and manage their account.

projects.title: Newsmatics Globe
projects.description: Hackathon project built for Newsmatics. A geolocation pipeline that extracts location data from news articles and visualizes them on an interactive 3D globe map.
projects.blogContent:
Newsmatics Globe was built during a hackathon organized by Newsmatics. The challenge was to create something innovative with news article database API.

I built a full geolocation pipeline that takes raw news articles, extracts location references using NLP (Natural Language Processing), matches the extracted location names with GeoNames database which features all the names of locations and their coordinates, then the article, locations and coordinates are validated using AI and the most probable location and coordinates are assigned to an article.

The extracted locations are then visualized as points on an interactive 3D globe rendered on a Globe.gl visualization where users can spin the globe, zoom in on hotspots, and click on individual articles.

The result is an OSINT style visual representation of where global news are happening in real time. You can literally see news clusters forming around conflict zones, political events, and natural disasters. It is also possible to playback the timeline of specific events as the news about them are being published. For example you could search for 'Ice Raid', select the date range (1st February up to 28th February 2024) and click on 'Play Timeline' to see the news about the Ice Raids as they were being published with their locations on the globe.

The goal of the project was to add another dimension to the vast amount of data that is in the Newsmatics database. The project demonstrates the power of combining NLP with geographic visualization, turning unstructured text data into spatial insights.

projects.title: BookReader for Even Realities G1
projects.description: An app for reading books on Even Realities G1 extended reality glasses, built on MentaOS (open-source OS for XR glasses). Allows users to upload books, read them on the glasses, and tracks reading progress and bookmarks.
projects.blogContent:
BookReader is an application I developed for the Even Realities G1 extended reality glasses. It runs on MentaOS, an open-source operating system designed specifically for XR glasses.

The app lets users upload their books and read them directly through the XR glasses display. It's designed for a seamless reading experience — you can read hands-free while commuting, exercising, or just lounging around.

Key features include automatic progress tracking so you never lose your place, a bookmarking system for saving important passages, and an optimized text rendering pipeline that ensures comfortable reading on the small XR display.

Building for XR glasses presented unique challenges: the display is tiny, input methods are limited, and battery life is a concern. I optimized the rendering to be lightweight and designed the UI to work with the glasses' limited input capabilities.

This project sits at the intersection of consumer hardware and open-source software, demonstrating how XR glasses can be used for practical everyday tasks beyond gaming and entertainment.

projects.title: PsycheTab
projects.description: A Chrome extension that replaces your new tab page with rotating, user-built collage backgrounds. Build aesthetic collages from your own images with drag, resize, and rotate controls.
projects.blogContent:
PsycheTab is a Chrome extension I built for myself, since I mainly use booksmarks and the search bar to navigate the web I found myself not making use of the widgets that the default 'New Tab' page provides also I am a big fan of displaying images that I find inspiring or whose vibe I resonate with.

The extension features a full collage builder where you can upload images to your local library, then drag them onto a canvas. Each image can be moved, resized from corners, rotated using a top handle, and reordered in the layer stack with right-click controls.

I personally use it to display screenshots of tweets that inspire me, and images whose essence I would like to integrate into my Psyche.

The extension uses vanilla HTML/CSS/JS for maximum performance, and stores everything locally in your browser using LocalForage (IndexedDB). No data ever leaves your machine. It's completely private.

Features include an image editor, image library and a scheduler for automatic rotation.

projects.title: NasadClaw
projects.description: Professional deployment and management of OpenClaw, an open-source AI assistant for leaders and teams. Localized for Czech & Slovak markets.
projects.blogContent:
NasadClaw is a website through which I offer OpenClaw installations and integrations. NasadClaw functions as professional B2B service focused on deploying and maintaining the OpenClaw AI assistant for executives and teams in the Czech and Slovak markets.

OpenClaw is an open-source AI agent that connects to over 50 services. However, setting it up securely and integrating it efficiently requires significant technical effort. That's where NasadClaw comes in: we provide a 'Collison Install' service where we personally deploy the AI agent on a dedicated Mac Mini or other hardware directly at the client's office.

The service ensures that strict InfoSec requirements are met, credentials are appropriately managed, and the system is rigorously hardened. By handling all complex deployments, security patches, and ongoing maintenance, teams get immediate leverage from day one instead of dealing with another software project to maintain.

The website itself was built using Next.js and Tailwind CSS, featuring a complete billing process through Stripe, and website analytics using PostHog. The design prioritizes speed, security, and a premium professional aesthetic to build trust with local enterprise clients.
